prompt: Design a Motorsport Event Operations and Asset Settlement Backend to manage offline race events end-to-end, from event configuration through officiating results and post-event billing, with audit-grade traceability. The service exposes resource-scoped API capabilities for Authentication (local username/password login, session issuance, password rotation), Roles and Permissions (Administrator, Event Director, Referee, Finance Clerk, Auditor; least-privilege access with role-to-resource actions), Competition Configuration (create/update versioned event templates and schedule groups; bind venues/equipment identifiers; publish/rollback rulesets), Results and Arbitration (capture results by event type: timing in milliseconds, distance in feet/inches, scoring as integers; multi-referee review requiring at least 2 reviewers for championship classes; arbitration by Event Director resolves conflicts; automatic ranking with deterministic tie-breakers: best attempt, then earliest valid attempt time; advancement computed from rule parameters such as top N or percentile; corrections follow request→review→effective with immutable prior versions; supports export-ready result datasets), Asset Ledger (maintain classified assets with coding rules, owning unit, responsible person, status enums such as InService/OutForRepair/Retired, procurement cost and date, useful life in months, straight-line depreciation parameters; bulk import/export with required-field validation, deduplication by asset code + serial/VIN, and full version history), and Vehicle Lifecycle (VIN required and unique, mileage non-decreasing, title transfer count integer ≥0; Draft→Published→Delisted→Sold workflow with mandatory reason for delist/sale and change audit). Billing and reconciliation APIs generate invoices and internal receipts for per-use, per-duration, or package pricing; discounts support percentage (0–30%) or fixed amount with a per-invoice cap of $500.00; refunds are recorded as adjustment lines against the original invoice and require Finance Clerk plus Auditor approval for amounts over $1,000.00. Because the system must run fully offline, “payment integration” is implemented as manual payment ledger entries (cash/check/ACH reference fields) and exception transaction handling (void, reversal, dispute) with idempotent externalReference per transaction to prevent duplicates. The backend uses Rocket for HTTP routing and request validation, SeaORM for persistence and migrations, and SQLite as the single-node database to run standalone in Docker without network dependencies. Core tables include users (unique username, salted password hash, last_login_at), roles, role_assignments, events (schedule_group, status, published_version), ruleset_versions (semantic version string, effective_at, created_by, rollback_of), results (event_id, participant_id, attempt_no, value_numeric, unit_enum, entered_by, reviewed_state), result_reviews (referee_id, decision enum, comment), assets (asset_code unique, category enum, brand, model, depreciation_months), vehicles (vin unique, registration_id, status enum), invoices (invoice_no unique, counterparty, issue_date, subtotal/tax/total), invoice_lines (pricing_model enum, quantity, unit_price, adjustment_type), and payment_entries (method enum, amount, received_at, externalReference unique). Data analytics capabilities provide a unified metric catalog (metric name unique, definition text, version, owner, audit trail) and multidimensional query interfaces supporting time-window filters, drill-down dimensions (event, class, venue, asset category), trend/funnel/retention computations, and CSV export; a data-quality module scans for missing required fields, outliers using configurable z-score thresholds (default 3.0), and duplicate-question-style detection generalized as duplicate record detection via stable hashing of selected fields. Security requirements include AES-256 encryption at rest for sensitive fields (payment references, personal identifiers) with key loaded from local environment, masking in audit exports, and an append-only audit log capturing who/what/when for all state transitions. Non-functional targets: p95 API latency under 250 ms on a single machine with 50 concurrent requests, nightly local backup rotation (7 days), and structured logs plus request correlation IDs for offline troubleshooting.
